page: Amazon Web Services a1.2xlarge is a first-generation ARM-based server powered by the AWS Graviton processor. It features 8 dedicated physical cores built on the ARM64 Cortex-A72 architecture, operating at a clock speed of 2.3 GHz. The instance is equipped with 16.0 GB of system memory, yielding a ratio of 2.0 GB per core. It runs on the Nitro hypervisor, includes 2 Gbps of baseline network bandwidth, and does not bundle local storage or GPU hardware.

Performance benchmarks indicate moderate capabilities for multi-threaded tasks but limitations in memory throughput and single-core execution. The server achieves average results in stress-ng single-core (1491.99) and multi-core (11904.08) tests, alongside an average score of 2462.51 in PassMark database operations. However, memory bandwidth is weak, with large-block reads registering at 23.51 GB/sec and writes at 8.88 GB/sec. Single-core performance is also low, as reflected by a Geekbench single-core score of 410.0 and a single-threaded gzip compression speed of 9.83 GB/sec.

The a1.2xlarge offers a cost-effective profile for environments transitioning to or testing on ARM64 architecture. Due to its lower memory bandwidth and modest single-core processing power, it is not designed for high-performance computing or memory-intensive databases. Instead, it serves as an efficient platform for scale-out workloads, containerized microservices, lightweight web serving, and basic development or testing pipelines.
description: Amazon Web Services a1.2xlarge is an ARM64 server powered by an 8-core AWS Graviton Cortex-A72 processor running at 2.3 GHz. Built on the Nitro hypervisor, it features 16.0 GB of RAM, 2 Gbps baseline network bandwidth, and no local storage or GPU. Benchmarks indicate average multi-core CPU performance and database operations, though memory bandwidth and single-core processing are relatively weak. This instance provides a cost-effective option for ARM64 application development, lightweight web serving, and containerized microservices.
